title: ENVs with `.`in the name do not take effect during the build process
body:
### Issue Description

`ENV` with `.` in the name do not take effect during the build process.
1. In `RUN` it will appear that the variable does not exist.
2. In ENV it will result in an error.

The problem only occurs during image builds, every environment with `.` defined in `Containerfile` works fine during container running.
The situation exists on both podman 4.9.5 on fedora39 and 5.1.1 on fedora40.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. build using follow `Containerfile`
```
FROM ubuntu
ENV test=1 \
    test.1=1
RUN env
```
2. build using follow `Containerfile`
```
FROM ubuntu
ENV test=1 \
    test.1=1
ENV A=${test}
RUN env
ENV B=${test.1}
RUN env
```

### Describe the results you received

1.
```
$ podman build -f ./Containerfile --no-cache
STEP 1/3: FROM ubuntu
STEP 2/3: ENV test=1     test.1=1
--> 7d847b2a502e
STEP 3/3: RUN env
test=1
HOSTNAME=5947f1c6a747
HOME=/root
PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
PWD=/
COMMIT
--> 35825aee1fe4
35825aee1fe4137b57e0d496b0ab4a5bd60756069d5381bc1e993eb8fad4d588
```
2.
```
$ podman build -f ./Containerfile.1 --no-cache
STEP 1/6: FROM ubuntu
STEP 2/6: ENV test=1     test.1=1
--> 27922bcccb3c
STEP 3/6: ENV A=${test}
--> b4afb91c4c21
STEP 4/6: RUN env
test=1
HOSTNAME=854e1f35cee7
HOME=/root
A=1
PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
PWD=/
--> 9802cc110ca8
Error: resolving step {Value:env Next:0xc000186fc0 Children:[] Heredocs:[] Attributes:map[] Original:ENV B=${test.1} Flags:[] StartLine:8 EndLine:8}: Missing ':' in substitution: ${test.1}
```

### Describe the results you expected

1. Output the following in STEP 3
```
test=1
test.1=1
HOSTNAME=5947f1c6a747
HOME=/root
PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
PWD=/
```
2. complete the build and add ENV `B=1`

### podman info output

```yaml
podman version

Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.1.1
API Version:  5.1.1
Go Version:   go1.22.3
Built:        Tue Jun  4 08:00:00 2024
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
Looking how Docker handles this, the second ENV is invalid.
```
# docker build --no-cache -f /tmp/Containerfile.1 /tmp
[+] Building 1.3s (2/2) FINISHED                                                                                                                                                                                               docker:default
 => [internal] load build definition from Containerfile.1                                                                                                                                                                                0.0s
 => => transferring dockerfile: 184B                                                                                                                                                                                                     0.0s
 => [internal] load metadata for docker.io/library/ubuntu:latest                                                                                                                                                                         1.3s
Containerfile.1:6
--------------------
   4 |     ENV A=${test}
   5 |     RUN env
   6 | >>> ENV B=${test.1}
   7 |     RUN env
   8 |     
--------------------
ERROR: failed to solve: failed to process "${test.1}": unsupported modifier (.) in substitution
```

Sorry, using an env with `.` in its name to assign a new env value seem to be unsupported in docker.
And docker can't handle env with `.` during build neither.

`# cat ./Containerfile`
```
FROM ubuntu
ENV test=1 \
    test.1=1
RUN env
CMD ["bash", "-c", "env"]
```

`# docker build -f ./Containerfile --progress=plain --no-cache .`
```
#0 building with "default" instance using docker driver

#1 [internal] load build definition from Containerfile
#1 transferring dockerfile: 112B done
#1 DONE 0.0s

#2 [internal] load metadata for docker.io/library/ubuntu:latest
#2 DONE 1.9s

#3 [internal] load .dockerignore
#3 transferring context: 2B done
#3 DONE 0.0s

#4 [1/2] FROM docker.io/library/ubuntu:latest@sha256:2e863c44b718727c860746568e1d54afd13b2fa71b160f5cd9058fc436217b30
#4 CACHED

#5 [2/2] RUN env
#5 0.123 test=1        <---
#5 0.123 HOME=/root
#5 0.123 PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
#5 0.123 PWD=/
#5 DONE 0.1s

#6 exporting to image
#6 exporting layers done
#6 writing image sha256:732248abeb9b50d7224908871816c4402b62e36747c66b48ed514ada7c6ac9bf done
#6 DONE 0.0s
```

`# docker run --rm 732248abeb9b5`
```
test.1=1      <---
HOSTNAME=b2636ae8c38b
PWD=/
HOME=/root
SHLVL=0
PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
test=1        <---
_=/usr/bin/env
```

But Env with `.` are available during container running, in podman too. Is it possible in build?

Ok so this seems to be the issue
```
$cat /tmp/Containerfile
FROM alpine
env test.1=abc
RUN printenv test.1
RUN echo ${test.1}
```

```
 $ podman build /tmp
STEP 1/4: FROM alpine
STEP 2/4: env test.1=abc
--> Using cache d4fedde0c416314107a2984dde24a9869a372cc3308d31384a3d30490ee65956
--> d4fedde0c416
STEP 3/4: RUN printenv test.1
abc
--> 63bdf56861b4
STEP 4/4: RUN echo ${test.1}
/bin/sh: syntax error: bad substitution
Error: building at STEP "RUN echo ${test.1}": while running runtime: exit status 2
```
@nalind was this something that you handled in imagebuilder recently?

> STEP 4/4: RUN echo ${test.1}
/bin/sh: syntax error: bad substitution
Error: building at STEP "RUN echo ${test.1}": while running runtime: exit status 2

The problem at this step is that `sh` does not accept variable names with dots in them.

I tried building from other images separately, and the results were inconsistent.

Containerfile
```
FROM alpine
ENV test.1=abc
RUN env
```

```
$ podman build -f Containerfile --no-cache .
STEP 1/3: FROM alpine
STEP 2/3: ENV test.1=adb
--> d8a909341cb5
STEP 3/3: RUN env
HOSTNAME=8b2237c3ab69
SHLVL=1
HOME=/root
test.1=adb        <--------
PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
PWD=/
COMMIT
--> a420bdea583b
a420bdea583b61a22bccbbdb7feb2c7485786261a468175cddb86ccd556e9126

$ podman build -f Containerfile --no-cache --from docker.io/ubuntu .
STEP 1/3: FROM docker.io/ubuntu
STEP 2/3: ENV test.1=adb
--> ac2d1045d65a
STEP 3/3: RUN env
HOSTNAME=0d7e2194d00f
HOME=/root
PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
PWD=/
COMMIT
--> 76aab2509760
76aab2509760c07956a676da1cd7e1f34900adb50fdca5c49b6b24664cb03c7e

$ podman build -f Containerfile --no-cache --from docker.io/debian .
STEP 1/3: FROM docker.io/debian
STEP 2/3: ENV test.1=adb
--> 0f5720bf79ed
STEP 3/3: RUN env
HOSTNAME=dee4f85d60b2
HOME=/root
PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
PWD=/
COMMIT
--> b3f0a644ffc8
b3f0a644ffc8935ef028ea55c6ef9cd1a7d99b33866dd167e81589c5d3858281

$ podman build -f Containerfile --no-cache --from docker.io/fedora .
STEP 1/3: FROM docker.io/fedora
STEP 2/3: ENV test.1=adb
--> 1a8d0f147646
STEP 3/3: RUN env
test.1=adb        <--------
HOSTNAME=d5150e4c60e5
DISTTAG=f40container
PWD=/
FBR=f40
HOME=/root
FGC=f40
SHLVL=0
PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
_=/usr/bin/env
COMMIT
--> e2d632028734
e2d632028734e1303303e8bf8fc490a050b47ec9a4e0a75ded8548ede3c2ad20

```

Seems odd, I tried `sh` and `bash` in the container separately.
```
$ podman run --rm b3f0a644ffc8 sh -c "env"
HOSTNAME=73325b27a67c
HOME=/root
container=podman
PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
PWD=/

$ podman run --rm b3f0a644ffc8 bash -c "env"
test.1=adb
HOSTNAME=155bb8b6b77e
PWD=/
container=podman
HOME=/root
SHLVL=0
PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
_=/usr/bin/env
```

So, looks like this isn't `podman`'s problem, it's `sh`'s.
However, unless using the docker format, during building I cannot use `SHELL` to switch to another shell. Using `bash -c` in `RUN` is meaningless.
So can I close this as Not a Podman issue?
sure. sorry for submitting the wrong issue without double checking.

> @nalind was this something that you handled in imagebuilder recently?

No, that was about handling substitutions that used /, %, or #.  As for environment variables with "." in their names, at this point I think we've confirmed that they'll be ignored or rejected in multiple places, much like :, /, %, and #, which are all given special treatment by the shell.  While environ(7) doesn't prohibit these characters, dash(1) suggests that "Variables set by the user must have a name consisting solely of alphabetics, numerics, and underscores - the first of which must not be numeric.", which is probably a good guideline.
